environment.supplementaryLocalChecks: macOS arm64, .NET SDK 10.0.301, EnableWindowsTargeting=true; local test execution not possible because the WPF test assembly requires the Windows Desktop runtime

confirmedBehavior: WAM is supported on Windows Server 2019 and later; MSAL automatically falls back to a browser when WAM cannot be used.
WithBroker(BrokerOptions) with a parent window handle is the required WAM integration; interactive sign-in is user-initiated; silent acquisition is attempted before interactive.

Microsoft.Identity.Client 4.61.0 and later requires the Microsoft.Identity.Client.Broker package for net-windows targets; this build uses Microsoft.Identity.Client and Microsoft.Identity.Client.Broker 4.86.1.
WAM requires an interactive Windows user session; this application has no service or scheduled mode.
The broker redirect URI (ms-appx-web://microsoft.aad.brokerplugin/{client_id}) is an app-registration deployment value and remains external to this repository.
microsoftPlatformReview.packageApiVerification: The exact MSAL 4.86.1 API surface (IsBrokerAvailable, WithBroker, MsalUiRequiredException.Classification, SubErrorForLogging, AccountId.TenantId/ObjectId) was verified against the restored package assemblies, not assumed from memory.

validatedActions.action: Release build and static analysis on Windows
validatedActions.details: 0 warnings, 0 errors. .NET analyzers enabled; warnings treated as errors in CI.

Coverage: configuration validation, tenant match/mismatch, guest-account rejection, operator allowlist success/rejection/empty, WAM-preferred selection, system-browser fallback, silent-token success and interaction-required, cancellation and consent classification, 401/403 classification, truthful sign-out semantics, DPAPI cache round-trip/tamper/corruption/reset-safety, cache-file ACL restriction, persistence-disabled behavior, log redaction, DI registration including absence of later-phase services, view-model sign-in/sign-out/remember/loading/error states, no employee-password surface, no prohibited authentication flows, and no M3+ employee-resolution or transfer behavior.

47 source and build files scanned for 15 prohibited patterns (Graph beta, application permissions, write permissions, ROPC-style acquisition, device-code flow, client secrets, certificates, employee-password UI). No matches.
Gitleaks reported: no leaks found. No real tenant values, client IDs, account IDs, or credentials were committed; appsettings.example.json contains placeholders only.

The only Microsoft Graph endpoint referenced in source is the approved operator /me endpoint (GRAPH-AUTH-001). No employee UPN resolution, drive resolution, delta inventory, scan, transfer, destination binding, resume, or report behavior exists.

earlierFailedRuns.note: Commit e15b78d failed restore: NU1510 warning-as-error for the redundant System.Security.Cryptography.ProtectedData package (inbox in .NET 10). Removed in a95bbd2.
Commit a95bbd2 failed 2 tests: an obsolete M1 lock-file assertion (MSAL is the approved M2 dependency) and a test harness that bypassed options validation. Fixed in a1afd83.

exercisedEndpointTemplates: GET https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/me?$select=id,userPrincipalName,displayName (validated via stubbed HTTP handler; no live tenant call)

statusConclusion.completionLabel: Source Implementation Complete remains unearned (later milestones pending)

unexecutedChecks: actual Microsoft interactive sign-in with a real authorized transfer account (M7)
WAM behavior and controlled browser fallback on Windows Server 2019 (M7)
MFA and Conditional Access challenge handling against a real tenant (M7)
limitations: All sign-in flows are unit-tested behind a test double for the MSAL boundary; no live tenant authentication was performed or claimed.
The DPAPI cache protector is exercised on the Windows CI runner; interactive WAM and system-browser experiences remain M7 production-acceptance checks.
The broker redirect URI and approved app registration are deployment values external to this repository (docs/ENVIRONMENT_AND_INPUTS.md).
Local macOS checks were supplementary only; mandatory evidence comes from Windows CI on the exact validated commit.
